input_files_consumed: risk_factors.json (46 risks: 5 CRITICAL, 10 HIGH, 29 MEDIUM, 2 LOW)

objectives: Establish Chris McGinley as the credibility anchor before any product claim is made (CCR-001 mitigation)
Deploy the 'Let It Fall' demonstration as the lead proof mechanism across all channels
Coin and embed 'face drift' vocabulary in all messaging to own the root cause naming
Position Forward Weighting / Face Down Balance as a NEW category — not another zero-torque variant
Launch beta tester program to begin closing the proof gap (CCR-002 mitigation)
Set up measurement infrastructure to capture launch data for Phase 2 optimization

actions.description: Produce the Chris McGinley origin story — a documentary-style long-form video (8-12 min) and condensed 60-second cutdown showing McGinley's 25-year OEM career, his design work for 11 world #1 players, and the engineering problem he set out to solve with forward weighting. This is not a product pitch — it is a credibility foundation. McGinley explains the physics in his own words. Show him in the engineering environment. Lead with the engineer, not the brand.
dependencies: Access to Chris McGinley for filming
Engineering facility or workshop for B-roll
actions.success_criteria: Video completed and approved before any product-focused content launches. McGinley's credentials are verifiable and no fabricated claims are present. Audience recognizes McGinley as the authority, not Performance Golf.
actions.resources_needed: Video production crew, McGinley's time (1-2 day shoot), post-production editing, engineering facility location

actions.description: Create the 'Let It Fall' hero demonstration video — the definitive side-by-side comparison showing three putters held at lie angle and released: (1) traditional putter — face twists open, (2) LAB-style ZT putter — face hangs neutral, (3) RS1 — face falls DOWN. This 15-30 second demonstration is the single most powerful proof asset. Produce in multiple formats: full demo, 6-second social cut, GIF, and still frame sequence. The evolutionary narrative (Traditional → LAB → RS1) is told visually in under 15 seconds.
dependencies: ZT putter for comparison (Bettinardi Antidote or similar — avoid using LAB directly to prevent antagonism)
actions.success_criteria: Demo clearly shows three distinct behaviors. No verbal claims needed — the physics speaks. Video is shareable, loopable, and works without sound. Produced in 16:9, 9:16, 1:1 formats for cross-platform deployment.
actions.resources_needed: High-quality camera setup (ideally slow-motion capable), clean background, three putters, production editing

actions.description: Write the core messaging document that embeds 'face drift' as the named root cause across all RS1 communications. This document defines the vocabulary hierarchy: (1) 'Face drift' — what happens (consumer-friendly root cause), (2) 'Forward Weighting' — what fixes it (the mechanism), (3) 'Face Down Balance' — how you can see it (the proof), (4) 'Roll Straight Technology' — the system name (7-feature integrated system). All copy, scripts, ads, emails, and landing pages must use this vocabulary consistently. No writer may substitute 'zero torque,' 'face balanced,' or 'face stable' for RS1-specific terms.
dependencies: Final product positioning approval from stakeholders
actions.success_criteria: All launch content uses RS1 vocabulary exclusively. Forum monitoring shows 'face drift' and 'forward weighting' appearing in audience discussions within 30 days of launch. No RS1 content uses competitor vocabulary ('zero torque,' 'lie angle balance').
actions.resources_needed: Senior copywriter, brand guidelines document, copy review checklist for all content creators

actions.description: Write the self-blame liberation messaging sequence — 'Your stroke was never the problem. Your putter's center of gravity was.' This is the emotional hook for the 32% Problem-Aware segment (461 quotes). Create three versions: (1) long-form for VSL opening (2-3 minutes establishing the identity crisis), (2) 30-second social ad targeting 'I can't putt' audience, (3) email subject line and opening for warm list. Every version must pair the liberation claim with the face-down balance proof — the claim alone will be dismissed without the demo.
dependencies: ACT-I-002 (demo video must be available for pairing)
actions.success_criteria: Messaging resonates with Problem-Aware segment without alienating golfers whose strokes genuinely need work. The liberation reframe is paired with proof in every instance. No version makes the claim without the demo.
actions.resources_needed: Direct response copywriter, video editor for demo integration, email platform setup

actions.description: Produce the evolutionary narrative content piece — 'The Three Generations of Putting Technology.' This tells the full story: (1) Traditional putters — CG behind shaft, face drifts open, golfer compensates, (2) LAB Golf / Zero Torque — CG aligned with shaft, face hangs neutral, passive stability (a genuine innovation), (3) RS1 / Forward Weighting — CG in front of shaft, face falls square, active face control (the next evolution). Respectful of LAB's contribution. Clear on why forward weighting is the next chapter, not a competitor. Create as long-form article, video script, and infographic.
actions.success_criteria: Narrative positions RS1 as evolution, not attack. LAB's innovation is acknowledged. The distinction between passive (ZT) and active (FW) is clear and memorable. Audience understands the three-generation arc.
actions.resources_needed: Content writer, infographic designer, physics visualization (CG diagrams for each generation)

actions.description: Create the honeymoon naming content — 'Why Every Putter Works for Two Weeks (And What Finally Changed).' This proactively names the honeymoon effect, explains WHY it happens (confidence boost fades, rearward CG reasserts), and positions forward weighting as the structural solution where the physics are permanent. Must include the line: 'Gravity does not have a honeymoon period.' Produce as email sequence opening, social ad concept, and VSL section.
actions.success_criteria: Content acknowledges the honeymoon pattern without triggering buyer concern that RS1 itself might honeymoon. The physics-based explanation is clear and the distinction from confidence-based improvement is unmistakable.
actions.resources_needed: Direct response copywriter, behavioral science reference for honeymoon explanation

actions.description: Launch the RS1 Beta Tester Program — recruit 50-100 golfers across handicap levels (5-20 handicap range, primary WEB demographic). Require Arccos or ShotScope tracking for minimum 10 rounds pre-RS1 and 10 rounds post-RS1. Track: putts per round, strokes gained putting, 3-putt frequency, make rate inside 6 feet. Collect video testimonials at 10, 20, and 30 rounds. This is the #1 proof gap closure action — without real-world user data, RS1 relies entirely on engineering claims.
actions.success_criteria: 50+ beta testers enrolled and tracking before launch week. First 10-round results available by Week 4. 30-round results available by Week 8. Video testimonials captured at each milestone.
actions.resources_needed: 50-100 RS1 units for beta program, Arccos/ShotScope licenses, beta program coordinator, video capture kit for testimonials

actions.description: Submit RS1 to MyGolfSpy for independent Most Wanted testing and commission SAM PuttLab analysis. Prepare a data package with each submission including product specs, forward CG measurement data, and the specific face-angle stability claims to be tested. Request face-angle-through-impact-zone testing specifically — this is the metric where RS1's forward weighting should demonstrably outperform. This action has a 6-12 month timeline but must be INITIATED in Phase 1.
actions.success_criteria: RS1 submitted to MyGolfSpy and SAM PuttLab within 2 weeks of launch. Testing timeline confirmed. Results expected within 6 months.
actions.resources_needed: RS1 units for submission, product data package, SAM PuttLab testing facility partnership

actions.description: Seed RS1 to 10-15 golf YouTube creators and podcast hosts for independent review. Prioritize creators who have previously reviewed LAB Golf (creates natural comparison content). Include the 'Let It Fall' demo protocol, McGinley's credentials summary, and the three-putter comparison setup. Target list: TXG, Rick Shiels, Ian Fraser, Let's Play Thru, Crossfield, MyGolfSpy (written), Plugged In Golf (written). Each creator receives: RS1 unit, comparison demo protocol, McGinley background document, and zero editorial constraints.
actions.success_criteria: 10+ creators agree to review. First reviews published within 4-6 weeks of launch. Reviews naturally feature the 'Let It Fall' demonstration. No fabricated positive coverage — let the product earn its reviews.
actions.resources_needed: 10-15 RS1 units, shipping, creator outreach coordinator, demo protocol document, McGinley credentials summary

actions.description: Deploy launch email sequence to Performance Golf's warm list. Sequence structure: (1) McGinley origin story email (credibility first), (2) 'Face drift' problem education email (name the enemy), (3) 'Let It Fall' demo video email (mechanism proof), (4) Evolutionary narrative email (Traditional → LAB → RS1), (5) Self-blame liberation email (your stroke was fine), (6) LAB comparison email (face stability without the tradeoffs), (7) Price reveal and offer email. Each email must use RS1 vocabulary exclusively. PG's warm list is the lowest-risk, highest-intent audience segment.
dependencies: ACT-I-001 through ACT-I-006 (content assets must be produced)
actions.success_criteria: 7-email sequence written, approved, and scheduled. Open rates and CTR tracked per email. Conversion tracked through full funnel. Email 3 (demo video) should have highest engagement.
actions.resources_needed: Email copywriter, email platform (Klaviyo/ActiveCampaign), video hosting, landing page for each email

actions.description: Launch paid social campaign with three ad concepts targeting different awareness levels: (1) Problem-Aware ad: 'You hit the ball like a scratch golfer. You putt like a 15. The gap isn't skill — it's physics.' → demo video → landing page. (2) Solution-Aware ad: 'Zero torque removes the twist. Forward Weighting controls the face. There's a difference.' → three-putter comparison → landing page. (3) Product-Aware ad: 'Everything you wanted from LAB Golf, without the tradeoffs. Steel face. Heel shaft. No adaptation. $399.' → feature comparison → landing page. Each ad type targets a distinct audience segment with distinct messaging.
actions.success_criteria: Three distinct ad sets live at launch. Each ad type achieves minimum 1.5% CTR within first 7 days. A/B testing reveals which awareness-level messaging converts best for budget allocation in Phase 2.
actions.resources_needed: Media buyer, ad creative (video + static), landing page developer, tracking pixels configured, initial ad budget

actions.description: Publish the 'Active vs Passive Face Control' comparison framework as an educational piece — NOT an attack on LAB. This creates a new evaluation dimension that splits the face-stability market into two buckets: passive (ZT — removes twist, face hangs neutral) and active (FW — creates corrective force, face falls square). The educational piece includes the physics explanation, the visual side-by-side, and the implications for the golfer. Published as blog post, infographic, and social content series. Obsessive vocabulary discipline: NEVER call RS1 'zero torque' or 'face stable' — always 'forward weighted' and 'face-down balanced.'
actions.success_criteria: Comparison framework published and distributed. Forum discussions begin using 'active vs passive' language within 30 days. RS1 is NOT categorized as a ZT putter in any major forum thread. The new evaluation dimension is established.
actions.resources_needed: Content writer with physics understanding, infographic designer, social media manager

actions.description: Implement the vocabulary discipline enforcement system. Create a copy review checklist that flags any use of competitor vocabulary ('zero torque,' 'lie angle balance,' 'face stable,' 'face balanced') in RS1 content. All writers, social media managers, and customer service representatives must use RS1-specific vocabulary exclusively. This is the primary mitigation for CCR-004 (ZT Fatigue Spillover) — if the market groups RS1 with ZT putters, the positioning fails.
actions.success_criteria: Zero instances of competitor vocabulary in RS1 content across all channels. Quarterly audit passes with 100% compliance. Customer service team trained on vocabulary by launch day.
actions.resources_needed: Copy checklist document, training session for all content creators and customer service, quarterly audit process

actions.description: Configure full tracking infrastructure for launch: (1) UTM parameters on all channels and content pieces for attribution, (2) Post-purchase survey capturing: how they heard about RS1, what putter they're replacing, what convinced them to buy, what almost stopped them, (3) Forum monitoring for 'RS1,' 'face drift,' 'forward weighting,' 'face down balance' mentions on GolfWRX, Reddit r/golf, TheSandTrap, MyGolfSpy, (4) Return rate tracking at 30/60/90 day intervals (CCR-005 honeymoon monitoring), (5) Brand perception tracking for 'budget' or 'cheap' associations (R-011-01 monitoring).
actions.success_criteria: All tracking systems live before first sale. Post-purchase survey response rate exceeds 30%. Forum monitoring produces weekly report. Return rate dashboard updates daily.
actions.resources_needed: Analytics engineer, survey platform (Typeform/Google Forms), forum monitoring tool (Mention/Brandwatch), e-commerce platform analytics

actions.description: Establish the brand credibility firewall: de-emphasize 'Performance Golf' branding in cold traffic campaigns. Lead with 'Chris McGinley's RS1' or 'RS1 by McGinley' in cold ad creative. Reserve 'Performance Golf' branding for warm list communications where the brand already has trust. This directly mitigates CCR-001 (Brand Credibility Gap). The firewall is simple: cold traffic sees the engineer, warm traffic sees the brand.
actions.success_criteria: Cold traffic ads feature McGinley credentials prominently, PG branding minimal. A/B test: McGinley-led creative vs PG-led creative. If PG-aware audiences convert at significantly lower rates than PG-unaware, the firewall is validated.
actions.resources_needed: Updated ad creative templates, brand guideline addendum for cold vs warm traffic, A/B test setup

actions.description: Build the RS1 product landing page with schema-violation structure. Instead of standard Problem-Mechanism-Proof-Offer flow, lead with the 'Let It Fall' demonstration video (first 10 seconds — no brand intro, no setup, just the demo). Then: 'What you just saw is different physics. Here's why it matters.' Below-the-fold: McGinley credentials, face drift explanation, evolutionary narrative, LAB comparison, price reveal. This violates the standard DR funnel structure (OPP-012) by leading with demonstration instead of brand story, creating surprise and engagement that bypasses Stage 4 numbness.
actions.success_criteria: Landing page converts at or above industry benchmarks for premium golf equipment. Schema-violation opening (demo-first) A/B tested against standard opening (brand-first). If demo-first outperforms, adopt as default. If not, revert to standard.
actions.resources_needed: Landing page designer/developer, video hosting, copywriter, A/B testing platform

objectives: Deploy first beta tester results as real-world proof (10-round data)
Refine messaging based on launch data — which awareness level converts best
Activate Tier 2 opportunities: Technology + Technique, Distance Control, Price Wedge, Anxiety/Yips
Close proof gaps: first YouTube reviews published, SAM PuttLab testing in progress
Monitor competitive response and forum categorization of RS1
Collect and deploy first customer testimonials

actions.description: Deploy 10-round beta tester results package. At Week 4, the first cohort of 50+ beta testers will have 10 rounds of Arccos/ShotScope data. Compile: average putts-per-round improvement, strokes gained putting change, 3-putt frequency reduction, make rate inside 6 feet. Publish as data visualization infographic + video testimonials from 5-10 strongest results. This is the first real proof injection — transitioning from engineering claims to verified user results.
actions.success_criteria: 10-round data shows measurable improvement (minimum 1.5 putts/round average improvement). At least 5 compelling video testimonials captured. Data published within 48 hours of compilation.
actions.resources_needed: Data analyst, video editor, infographic designer, testimonial release forms

actions.description: Amplify first YouTube creator reviews as they publish. Clip key moments — particularly any 'Let It Fall' reactions and A/B putting comparisons against current gamers. Repurpose as: paid social ads (creator reaction clips), email content for warm list (creator review roundup), and landing page social proof section. Prioritize creator content that naturally features the three-putter comparison.
actions.success_criteria: Minimum 5 creator reviews published by Week 6. Creator review clips integrated into paid social within 48 hours of publication. Landing page updated with creator quote carousel.
actions.resources_needed: Social media manager, video editor, licensing agreements with creators for clip usage

actions.description: Conduct Week 4 launch data analysis and messaging optimization. Analyze: (1) Which awareness-level ad set converts best (Problem-Aware vs Solution-Aware vs Product-Aware), (2) Which email in the sequence has highest conversion rate, (3) Post-purchase survey data on what convinced buyers and what almost stopped them, (4) Forum categorization — is RS1 being grouped with ZT or established as its own category, (5) Return rate baseline at 30 days. Reallocate ad budget to highest-converting awareness level. Adjust messaging based on objections cited in post-purchase surveys.
actions.success_criteria: Data analysis completed within 3 days of Week 4 milestone. Budget reallocation executed by end of Week 4. Messaging adjustments deployed by Week 5. Forum monitoring confirms RS1 categorization status.
actions.resources_needed: Data analyst, media buyer, copywriter for messaging adjustments, forum monitoring report

actions.description: Create the LAB blocker-breaker comparison content for Product-Aware audience (26% of addressable market, 375 quotes). This is the direct RS1 vs LAB comparison that addresses all 5 documented LAB blockers: (1) Aesthetics — T-Wing mallet vs UFO shape, (2) Feel — CNC steel face vs aluminum, (3) Adaptation — works day one vs 2-8 week adjustment, (4) Shaft — heel-shaft option vs center-shaft only, (5) Price — $399/$429 vs $449-$649. Produce as comparison infographic, dedicated comparison landing page, and 2-minute video. Tone: respectful of LAB's innovation, clear on RS1's advantages. Never adversarial.
actions.success_criteria: Comparison content deployed to Product-Aware ad audience. Conversion rate from LAB-comparison landing page exceeds standard landing page. No negative LAB community reaction (monitoring required). Price positioned as validation, not headline.
actions.resources_needed: Copywriter, infographic designer, video producer, dedicated comparison landing page

actions.description: Develop Technology + Technique secondary messaging (OPP-008). Create content showing that RS1 both improves ball flight (face stability) AND trains better stroke mechanics over time (74-degree lie angle + gravity-driven pendulum). Position as SECONDARY benefit — 'In addition to keeping your face square, the RS1 actually improves your stroke mechanics over time.' Do NOT lead with this claim until SAM PuttLab data validates it. For now, frame as a user-reported benefit supported by physics theory.
dependencies: ACT-S-001 (beta tester qualitative feedback on stroke improvement)
actions.success_criteria: Dual-promise content created and deployed as supporting messaging in email sequence and landing page below-the-fold. Not featured in primary ad creative. Beta tester feedback validates the stroke improvement experience.
actions.resources_needed: Copywriter, beta tester interview data, 74-degree lie angle visualization

actions.description: Create the distance control advantage content (OPP-009). Address ZT's #1 documented complaint head-on: 'Face stability AND distance control. Not one or the other.' Explain the gravity-driven pendulum mechanism and the steel face advantage. Position as a blocker-remover rather than a superiority claim until testing data validates superiority. Produce as email content, social posts, and FAQ section on landing page.
actions.success_criteria: Distance control messaging deployed as supporting content. No headline-level distance control claims until testing data validates. Beta tester feedback on distance control incorporated.
actions.resources_needed: Copywriter, beta tester distance control data

actions.description: Develop the putting anxiety / yips nurture sequence (OPP-010). This targets the 10-15% of the addressable market experiencing clinical-level putting anxiety. Create a 5-email nurture sequence: (1) 'The doom loop explained — why pressure putts get harder, not easier,' (2) 'The mechanical antidote — when physics replaces guesswork, anxiety loses its grip,' (3) 'What happens when the face stays square without your help,' (4) Beta tester testimonial from anxiety sufferer, (5) Offer with anxiety-specific angle. This is nurture content, NOT primary launch messaging.
dependencies: ACT-S-001 (beta tester testimonials — specifically anxiety sufferers)
actions.success_criteria: Nurture sequence deployed to segmented audience showing anxiety/yips-related engagement signals. Conversion rate tracked separately from primary funnel. Positioned as supportive content, not 'problem putter' marketing.
actions.resources_needed: Copywriter with sensitivity to anxiety messaging, email segmentation setup, beta tester testimonial (anxiety-specific)

actions.description: Deploy 20-round beta tester results (Week 6-8). Update the proof package with sustained improvement data. The 20-round milestone is critical for the anti-honeymoon positioning — if improvement is sustained at 20 rounds, the 'gravity doesn't have a honeymoon period' claim gains its first empirical support. Compare 10-round and 20-round data to confirm no regression. If data shows sustained improvement, promote aggressively. If regression appears, immediately investigate and adjust claims.
actions.success_criteria: 20-round data shows improvement sustained or increasing from 10-round baseline. Zero regression signal. Published as 'Week 6 Update' content across all channels. Anti-honeymoon narrative gains first proof point.
actions.resources_needed: Data analyst, content creator, beta tester coordination

actions.description: Deploy the price wedge as supporting content (OPP-011). Now that primary messaging has established RS1's mechanism advantage, introduce price as a validation element: 'More technology for less money.' Create a value comparison table: RS1 ($399/$429) vs LAB DF3 ($449-$649) vs Spider ZT ($379) vs Scotty Cameron ($449-$479) vs Bettinardi Antidote ($449). Feature set comparison alongside price. Price must VALIDATE the technology story, never LEAD it.
actions.success_criteria: Price comparison content deployed in consideration-phase touchpoints (email sequence, landing page mid-section). No ad creative leads with price. Monitor for 'budget' or 'cheap' brand perception — if detected, reduce price messaging prominence.
actions.resources_needed: Copywriter, comparison table designer, monitoring setup for brand perception

actions.description: Launch high-speed camera face-angle-through-impact video content. Commission high-speed camera testing (2000+ fps) comparing RS1 vs traditional putter vs ZT putter face angle through the impact zone. Overlay angle measurements in real-time. This is the visual proof asset for the data-driven segment and creates viral-potential content for golf YouTube and social media. Produce as standalone content and as an embed within the existing landing page.
actions.success_criteria: High-speed video clearly shows RS1 face-angle advantage through impact zone. Video achieves strong engagement metrics on social media (shares, saves). Data-driven segment responds positively.
actions.resources_needed: High-speed camera facility, cameraman, angle measurement overlay software, three putters, golfer testers

actions.description: Collect and deploy first customer testimonials (non-beta buyers). At Week 3-4, reach out to early purchasers for testimonial collection. Track for the organic emergence of confidence language (OPP-015 monitoring trigger). If 60%+ of testimonials mention confidence unprompted, consider elevating it as a secondary theme. Prioritize testimonials that reference specific metrics (putts per round, strokes gained) and competitor switching stories ('I replaced my [X] and immediately...').
actions.success_criteria: Minimum 20 customer testimonials collected by Week 6. At least 5 with specific metrics. Confidence language tracking initiated. Testimonials deployed on landing page, email sequence, and social proof ads.
actions.resources_needed: Customer success team, testimonial collection platform, video recording guidance for remote testimonials

actions.description: Monitor and respond to competitive positioning. Track: (1) Forum categorization of RS1 — is it being grouped with ZT or established as its own category? If grouped with ZT, deploy corrective content emphasizing the active vs passive distinction. (2) Competitor response — any OEM announcing forward-CG or forward-weighted putter development. If detected, accelerate content saturation (CCR-003 mitigation). (3) LAB Golf pricing changes — if LAB drops prices, reduce price comparison messaging immediately. (4) ZT fatigue spillover — if audience cynicism targets RS1 as 'another face stability gimmick,' increase demonstration-led content.
actions.success_criteria: Weekly competitive intelligence report produced. RS1 categorized as 'forward weighted' (not 'zero torque') in 70%+ of forum mentions. No OEM competitor announcement detected. If any trigger fires, response deployed within 72 hours.
actions.resources_needed: Competitive intelligence analyst, forum monitoring tool, response playbook document

actions.description: Implement return rate early warning system (CCR-005 monitoring). Track returns at 30-day intervals segmented by: (1) reason cited, (2) customer handicap level, (3) putter replaced, (4) awareness level at purchase. If return rate exceeds 10% at any 30-day window OR if returns increase between 30 and 60 days (potential honeymoon regression signal), trigger immediate investigation. Cross-reference with beta tester longitudinal data to determine whether returns correlate with the regression pattern documented in CCR-005.
actions.success_criteria: Return rate dashboard live and updated daily. 30/60/90-day return rate data captured. No regression signal detected (return rate stable or declining over time). If regression detected, escalation protocol activated within 24 hours.
actions.resources_needed: E-commerce analytics, customer service team trained on return reason coding, escalation protocol document

objectives: Deploy independent testing results (MyGolfSpy, SAM PuttLab) as data proof package
Build the data-driven segment proof ecosystem (OPP-014)
Activate Stage 5 identity layer once mechanism credibility is established
Optimize based on 90-day customer data and 30-round beta tester results
Explore community building for RS1 owners

actions.description: Deploy independent testing results as the data proof package (OPP-014). By Month 3-6, MyGolfSpy and/or SAM PuttLab results should be available. If results show RS1 outperforms competitors on face-angle consistency, promote aggressively across all channels with specific numbers. If results show parity, frame as 'RS1 matches the best AND eliminates their tradeoffs.' If results are unfavorable on any metric, do NOT publish that metric — focus on metrics where RS1 excels. Create forward-weighting-specific metrics (face-down balance quantification, active vs passive face-squaring measurements) that competitors haven't tested.
actions.success_criteria: Independent testing results published with RS1 results favorable on face-angle metrics. Data proof package integrated into landing page, email sequence, and paid social. Data-driven segment engagement increases measurably.
actions.resources_needed: Data analyst, content creator for data visualization, landing page update, email sequence update

actions.description: Deploy 30-round and 60-round beta tester longitudinal data. This is the definitive anti-honeymoon proof. If improvement sustains through 60 rounds (3-4 months of regular play), the 'gravity doesn't have a honeymoon period' claim is empirically validated. Publish as a longitudinal study: 'RS1 Performance Over Time — 60 Rounds of Data.' Compare the RS1 trajectory (expected: sustained improvement) against the documented conventional putter trajectory (honeymoon → regression). This becomes RS1's most powerful long-term proof asset.
actions.success_criteria: 60-round data shows sustained or improving performance with no regression. 'No honeymoon regression' is published as a headline data point. Customer testimonials at 60 rounds reinforce 'can't go back' narrative.
actions.resources_needed: Data analyst, long-form content creator, beta tester coordination for 60-round milestone

actions.description: Begin professional player seeding program. Leverage Chris McGinley's OEM network to introduce RS1 to mini-tour, Korn Ferry Tour, Champions Tour, and LPGA players. Target 10-20 players across levels. Provide custom-fitted RS1 units with full fitting protocol. Track any competitive results. Do NOT fabricate or imply tour endorsement — any professional usage must be organic and verifiable. Even 3-5 visible professional users changes the credibility equation (proof_inventory.json gap #3).
dependencies: Custom fitting capability for professional players
actions.success_criteria: 10+ professional players have received and tested RS1. Any competitive results documented and shared (only if authentic). No fabricated tour claims.
actions.resources_needed: McGinley's network access, custom fitting services, 10-20 RS1 units, player outreach coordinator

actions.description: Deploy the comprehensive 'RS1 vs Everything' comparison guide. By Month 3-6, RS1 has beta data, customer testimonials, creator reviews, and potentially independent testing. Create a comprehensive comparison landing page: RS1 vs LAB DF3, RS1 vs Spider ZT, RS1 vs Scotty Cameron, RS1 vs Bettinardi Antidote, RS1 vs Evnroll. Each comparison addresses the specific competitive dynamics for that matchup. Tone: informative, not adversarial. Let data and features speak.
actions.success_criteria: Comparison page ranks for '[competitor] vs RS1' search queries. Organic traffic from comparison searches increases month over month. Each comparison page converts at or above main landing page rate.
actions.resources_needed: SEO-focused content writer, comparison page developer, competitive data compilation

actions.description: Accelerate content saturation to establish 'Forward Weighting = RS1' association as the defensive moat against OEM co-option (CCR-003). By Month 3-6, RS1 should own the forward weighting conversation so thoroughly that any OEM entering the space is seen as copying RS1, just as OEMs entering ZT were seen as copying LAB. Content volume targets: 50+ pieces of forward-weighting content published (articles, videos, social posts, email), 'face drift' vocabulary appearing in organic forum discussions without RS1 prompting, patent status publicized for Face Down Balance mechanism.
actions.success_criteria: 50+ forward-weighting content pieces published. 'Face drift' and 'forward weighting' appear in organic forum discussions. Patent status for FDB publicized. Any OEM entering forward-CG space would be perceived as following RS1.
actions.resources_needed: Content team (writer, video producer, social manager), patent attorney consultation, content calendar

actions.description: Launch the RS1 owner community and referral program. Build a mechanism for owners to share results (Arccos screenshots, putting round scores), get support, and refer friends. The 'Let It Fall' test is inherently social — owners demonstrate it to playing partners (Q0312: 'Someone asks to try my putter almost every round'). Amplify this natural virality with: (1) RS1 Scorecard tracking tool for owners, (2) 'Share your results' social media prompt with branded hashtag, (3) Referral discount for owner recommendations. Community becomes an organic testimonial engine.
actions.success_criteria: Community launched with 200+ active members. Organic social posts using RS1 branded hashtag increasing weekly. Referral program generating measurable new customer acquisition. Confidence language emerging organically in community discussions.
actions.resources_needed: Community platform (Facebook Group, dedicated forum, or app), community manager, referral program setup, branded scorecard tool

actions.description: Begin layering Stage 5 identity elements onto the established Stage 4 mechanism story (OPP-016). Once forward weighting credibility is established through proof (independent testing, user data, creator reviews), introduce identity framing: 'For golfers who trust physics over marketing.' This is NOT the primary message — it is a secondary layer for the 15-20% of the market that responds to identity-based positioning. Create identity-oriented content: the 'Forward Thinker' owner identity, the anti-identity contrast ('the guy who buys the latest shiny putter every year vs the golfer who bought the last putter he'll ever need').
dependencies: Mechanism credibility established (independent testing published, 100+ positive testimonials)
actions.success_criteria: Identity content deployed to segmented audience. A/B tested against mechanism-only content. If identity layer increases conversion, expand. If neutral or negative, maintain mechanism-only positioning. Identity content never replaces mechanism content — only layers on top.
actions.resources_needed: Brand strategist, copywriter, A/B testing setup

actions.description: Conduct 90-day comprehensive performance review. Analyze all data collected across Phases 1-2: (1) Total sales and revenue vs projections, (2) Customer acquisition cost by channel and awareness level, (3) Return rate trend at 30/60/90 days — is it stable, declining, or increasing, (4) Net Promoter Score from customer surveys, (5) Competitive response status — any OEM moves toward forward CG, (6) Forum sentiment analysis — positive/negative/neutral ratio, (7) Proof gap closure status — which of the 8 gaps are closed, which remain, (8) Beta tester longitudinal data trajectory. This review determines strategic direction for Months 6-12.
dependencies: All Phase 1 and Phase 2 actions completed or in progress
actions.success_criteria: Comprehensive review document produced with clear go/no-go recommendations for each messaging strategy. Return rate trend analysis confirms no honeymoon regression. Competitive landscape assessed for OEM response timeline. Proof gap closure timeline updated.
actions.resources_needed: Data analyst, strategy lead, competitive intelligence, customer insights team

actions.description: Establish RS1 fitting partnership with professional club fitters. Partner with 20+ respected club fitters who have SAM PuttLab access. Let them test RS1 with their clients and collect professional assessments. Fitter endorsements carry more weight than any ad with the data-driven segment (proof_inventory.json authority proof gap). Create a standardized RS1 fitting protocol that generates before/after comparison graphics. The fitting experience becomes a proof-generation engine — every fitting produces data that validates the mechanism.
dependencies: Production units available for fitting inventory
actions.success_criteria: 20+ fitters have RS1 in their fitting inventory. Fitter testimonials and before/after data collected from first 50+ fittings. Fitter endorsements deployed in marketing materials (with permission).
actions.resources_needed: 20+ RS1 fitting units, fitter outreach coordinator, SAM PuttLab fitting protocol document, data collection template

critical_path.description: The minimum set of actions that MUST happen for launch success — without these, RS1 risks being dismissed as another marketing-company putter with unsubstantiated claims in a Stage 4 market that reflexively ignores verbal mechanism claims.
critical_path.rationale: The critical path addresses the two existential vulnerabilities simultaneously: (1) ACT-I-001 (McGinley origin story) and ACT-I-015 (brand credibility firewall) mitigate CCR-001 (Performance Golf has zero hardware credibility) by putting the engineer — not the marketing company — at the front of every first impression. (2) ACT-I-002 (Let It Fall demo) is the single proof asset that bypasses both brand skepticism AND Stage 4 mechanism fatigue — it does not ask the audience to trust a claim, it asks them to watch physics. (3) ACT-I-003 (vocabulary document) and ACT-I-013 (vocabulary enforcement) ensure RS1 is never grouped with zero-torque competitors, preventing CCR-004 (ZT fatigue spillover). (4) ACT-I-007 (beta tester launch) and ACT-S-001/ACT-S-008 (10-round and 20-round results) begin closing CCR-002 (proof gap) within the first 30-60 days — without real user data, RS1 relies entirely on engineering claims in a market that demands proof. (5) ACT-I-010 (email sequence to warm list) deploys the full messaging stack to the lowest-risk, highest-intent audience first, generating initial sales and early testimonials that feed the broader launch. Every action on the critical path either establishes credibility the brand lacks, deploys proof the market demands, or protects the positioning from being lumped with exhausted competitors.

sequencing_rationale: The action sequence follows a Credibility-First, Proof-Second, Scale-Third logic that directly addresses RS1's unique market position: a genuinely differentiated product from a brand with zero hardware credibility entering a Stage 4 market drowning in mechanism claims. Phase 1 (Immediate) front-loads the two assets RS1 has at launch that no competitor can replicate: Chris McGinley's 25-year engineering pedigree and the face-down balance physical demonstration. These bypass the brand credibility gap (CCR-001) and Stage 4 mechanism fatigue simultaneously — McGinley is not a marketer making claims, and the demo is not a verbal claim to be dismissed. Phase 1 also coins and embeds 'face drift' vocabulary to own the root cause naming before any competitor can co-opt it, and launches the beta tester program that will produce the user proof RS1 desperately needs. Phase 2 (Short-Term, Weeks 2-8) pivots from engineering claims to empirical proof as beta tester data arrives. The 10-round and 20-round data milestones are sequenced to build the anti-honeymoon narrative incrementally — each milestone validates that improvement is sustained, not temporary. YouTube creator reviews amplify third-party validation. Tier 2 opportunities (Technology + Technique, distance control, price wedge, anxiety/yips) activate as secondary messaging now that the primary mechanism story is established. Phase 3 (Medium-Term, Months 3-6) shifts to competitive fortification and long-term proof. Independent testing results provide the data proof the data-driven segment requires. 60-round longitudinal data delivers the definitive anti-honeymoon evidence. Professional player seeding begins building tour-adjacent credibility. Content saturation establishes the 'Forward Weighting = RS1' association that protects against OEM co-option. Identity elements layer onto the mechanism story only after credibility is established — premature identity positioning without mechanism proof would feel hollow. Throughout all three phases, every Tier 1 opportunity has at least 2 actions in Phase 1, every CRITICAL and HIGH risk has a corresponding mitigation action, and proof gap closure is tracked against the 8 gaps identified in proof_inventory.json. The sequence is designed so that each phase's proof assets feed the next phase's messaging — beta data feeds testimonial content, creator reviews feed social proof, independent testing feeds data-driven campaigns, and longitudinal data feeds the anti-honeymoon narrative. No phase makes claims it cannot yet support.
